rationale: Both my round-1 RCs are fixed at eb6a2ed1. F3-1: R3 now pins enumeration fail-CLOSED (refuse naming the plan path on missing/corrupt plan.md), with a dedicated falsifier and AC6(e); it does not contradict R5 (the normal path presupposes a readable plan) or the pristine-bead no-op (metadata-content, not enumeration). F3-2: the inoperative Dolt-hiccup 'wedge finalize' clause is gone (zero wedge/hiccup hits); OQ2 now rests on shared-predicate parity + merge-gates-cannot-fail-open. The OQ2 asymmetry is internally consistent: R2's fail-open is confined to decorating an already-fired refusal (falsifier: corrupt panel must neither pass nor crash), so it cannot hide unsettlement, while R1/R3 fail closed on everything that could; R1's falsifier pins wrapper parity so complete/next/doctor stay byte-identical, consistent with Out-of-Scope. All 4 OQs [x] and mutually consistent; the only dodge-verb hit is a verbatim quoted code comment. Goal discloses both residuals. 4gsz reconciliation coherent (115 = the merging consumer; CI leg stays 4gsz). Discriminators verified: ':398' single-line comment (1 hit), 'closed without' 0/3 files, 'impl approve' 0 in ADR-0037, lifecycle claimed by workflow only. Scope still ~3 beads.

findings.note: R1 specs an error-preserving core (ScanOrphanedClosedBeads-style, returns ([]Orphan, error)) with FindOrphanedClosedBeads retained as byte-identical fail-open wrapper; OQ2 rewritten to the advisory-fail-open/gate-fail-closed asymmetry with explicit round-1-panel override; AC1(b) pins the infra-error refusal AND wrapper parity in internal/lifecycle tests. Coherent with R2/R5.

My F3-1. R3: 'Enumeration is fail-CLOSED' on the readPlanBeadIDs error cases (verified at impl.go:452; gate-1/3 silent skip verified at :226-227 'if planErr == nil'), refusal names the plan path, falsifier covers the silent-skip inheritance, AC6(e) is runnable. No conflict with R5 or the pristine no-op.

Refusal guarantee narrowed to no close/phase-write/merge/push with phase.EnsureMigrated (verified at impl.go:140) explicitly carved out as the pre-existing pre-gate migration step; Background's complete.Run ordering corrected (steps 1.6/2.25 before the step-2.5 auto-commit; 3.5/3.75 after it but pre-terminal).

R1 states epic-scoped detection citing orphans.go:75-83 (verified: findEpicBySpecIDFn then listClosedBeadsFn(epicID)); AC2(c) pins the different-spec-orphan negative; blp6 deferral explicit in Background, Out-of-Scope, Non-Goals, and as Goal residual #2. No contradiction with the untouched global merge loop — the residual is disclosed.

Scope + AC9 repoint to the binary-embedded lifecycleSkillFiles() literal (verified: func at internal/setup/claude.go:681, ms-impl-approve entry at :736) plus .claude/skills/ and .agents/skills/ copies; AC9 grep uses 'closed without' — verified 0 hits in all three files today, genuinely discriminating.

AC8 targets 'rule as CompleteBead' — verified sitting alone on mindspec_executor.go:398, exactly 1 occurrence in internal/executor, so both the removed-line diff grep and the post-change zero-count are single-line matchable.

AC10 gains the execution-file negative discriminator (stated FALSE at round-1 SHA; TRUE now that the ownership move is committed as part of this revision — as the brief intends); AC7 names TestRecoveryConvergence_AlreadyClosedOrphan with a grep existence check so the -run pattern cannot vacuously pass.

Verified at eb6a2ed1: internal/lifecycle/** present in .mindspec/domains/workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml:9, absent from execution OWNERSHIP.yaml (diff shows the -1/+1 move). Exactly one claimant; Impacted Domains and AC10 both point at workflow; execution paragraph explicitly notes the removal.

Goal coverage claim now qualified ('wherever its detection signal or a recorded obligation can see the gap') and names both residuals: the never-refuted raw-merged bead (no durable obligation) and cross-spec orphans on the global loop (blp6). R3's coverage is correspondingly qualified ('only when a durable obligation was recorded').

Zero 'wedge'/'hiccup' hits — the inoperative Dolt-hiccup rationale is gone from OQ2; orphans.go bound corrected to :74-112 (matches the verified function extent); Background reads tight with the quoted executor comment properly attributed.

Background frames 115 as the fourth (merging) consumer of the shared 4gsz predicate — complete/next/doctor legs shipped under 4gsz, the CI leg explicitly retained as 4gsz scope in Out-of-Scope. No contradiction with the Out-of-Scope list; no scope creep pulling other legs into 115.
